message:
refactor(http): return index bounds from header parser to avoid redundant UTF-8 checks

Follow-up to the byte-level parsing consolidation: rename
`parse_header_line_impl` to `parse_header_line_bounds` and return
`(colon, value_start, value_end)` index bounds instead of borrowed byte
slices. This eliminates the lifetime coupling between input and output,
and lets the `parse_header_line(&str)` path slice directly from the
original `&str` without a redundant `from_utf8()` round-trip on
already-validated UTF-8 input.

The `parse_header_line_bytes(&[u8])` path continues to use `from_utf8()`
on the extracted name and value byte ranges, which is correct since the
input bytes have no UTF-8 guarantee.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
